_metadata.title: Classification Rules for High-Risk AI Systems

primary_official_source.name: EUR-Lex -- Official Journal of the European Union

obligations.source_quote: Irrespective of whether an AI system is placed on the market or put into service independently of the products referred to in points (a) and (b), that AI system shall be considered to be high-risk where both of the following conditions are fulfilled:

decomposed_atoms.description: Art.6(1)(a): AI is safety component of product or is product itself, covered by Annex I
decomposed_atoms.requirement: System SHALL be considered high-risk if it is a safety component or product under Annex I harmonisation legislation

decomposed_atoms.description: Art.6(1)(b): Product requires third-party conformity assessment
decomposed_atoms.requirement: System SHALL be considered high-risk if the product requires third-party conformity assessment under Annex I

obligations.source_quote: In addition to the high-risk AI systems referred to in paragraph 1, AI systems referred to in Annex III shall be considered to be high-risk.

decomposed_atoms.description: Annex III AI systems are high-risk
decomposed_atoms.requirement: AI systems in Annex III categories SHALL be considered high-risk

obligations.source_quote: By derogation from paragraph 2, an AI system referred to in Annex III shall not be considered to be high-risk where it does not pose a significant risk of harm to the health, safety or fundamental rights of natural persons, including by not materially influencing the outcome of decision making.

decomposed_atoms.description: System does not pose significant risk
decomposed_atoms.requirement: System SHALL NOT be considered high-risk if it does not pose significant risk of harm

decomposed_atoms.description: Art.6(3)(a): intended for narrow procedural task
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Exception applies if system is intended to perform a narrow procedural task

decomposed_atoms.description: Art.6(3)(b): intended to improve prior human activity
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Exception applies if system improves result of previously completed human activity

decomposed_atoms.description: Art.6(3)(c): detects patterns without replacing human assessment
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Exception applies if system detects decision-making patterns without replacing human assessment
Exception applies if system performs a preparatory task to an assessment
System SHALL always be high-risk if it performs profiling of natural persons (no exception)

obligations.source_quote: A provider who considers that an AI system referred to in Annex III is not high-risk shall document its assessment before that system is placed on the market or put into service.

decomposed_atoms.requirement: Provider SHALL document the assessment that the system is not high-risk BEFORE placing on market
Provider SHALL be subject to registration obligation in Art. 49(2)

decomposed_atoms.description: Documentation available to authorities on request
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Provider SHALL provide documentation of assessment upon request of national competent authorities

obligations.source_quote: The Commission shall, after consulting the European Artificial Intelligence Board (the 'Board'), and no later than 2 February 2026, provide guidelines specifying the practical implementation of this Article in line with Article 96 together with a comprehensive list of practical examples of use cases of AI systems that are high-risk and not high-risk.
obligations.note: Commission obligation to provide guidelines by 2 February 2026. Not a provider obligation.

decomposed_atoms.requirement: Commission SHALL provide guidelines with practical examples by 2 February 2026

obligations.source_quote: The Commission is empowered to adopt delegated acts in accordance with Article 97 in order to amend paragraph 3, second subparagraph, of this Article by adding new conditions to those laid down therein, or by modifying them, where there is concrete and reliable evidence of the existence of AI systems that fall under the scope of Annex III, but do not pose a significant risk of harm to the health, safety or fundamental rights of natural persons.
obligations.note: Commission empowerment to add exception conditions. Not a provider obligation.

decomposed_atoms.description: Commission can add new exception conditions to Art.6(3)
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Commission is empowered to add or modify exception conditions in Art.6(3)

obligations.source_quote: The Commission shall adopt delegated acts in accordance with Article 97 in order to amend paragraph 3, second subparagraph, of this Article by deleting any of the conditions laid down therein, where there is concrete and reliable evidence that this is necessary to maintain the level of protection of health, safety and fundamental rights provided for by this Regulation.
obligations.note: Commission obligation to remove exception conditions when necessary. Not a provider obligation.

decomposed_atoms.description: Commission must remove conditions if evidence warrants
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Commission SHALL remove exception conditions when necessary to maintain protection level

obligations.source_quote: Any amendment to the conditions laid down in paragraph 3, second subparagraph, adopted in accordance with paragraphs 6 and 7 of this Article shall not decrease the overall level of protection of health, safety and fundamental rights provided for by this Regulation and shall ensure consistency with the delegated acts adopted pursuant to Article 7(1), and take account of market and technological developments.
obligations.note: Constraint on Commission delegated acts. Not a provider obligation.

decomposed_atoms.description: Amendments must not decrease protection level
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Amendments SHALL NOT decrease overall protection of health, safety, and fundamental rights
